claims.text: Stephen Andrews agrees with Mary Jo Cole that most people hire wedding planners because they don't want to plan, not because they want to co-plan.
claims.subject: Stephen Andrews
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: I agree with your mom. I think most people hire wedding planners because they DONT want to plan

claims.text: Stephen Andrews characterizes Catherine Cole's approach to wedding planning as using a planner to enhance her own planning — a 'planner steroid' — rather than delegating fully.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: You just want to enhance your planning with a planner steroid

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole prefers the wedding table design with fewer candles, finding the multi-candle version too crowded, unbalanced, and out of proportion.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I think the first photo, with fewer candles, looks better - the other one looks too crowded and a little…odd maybe?

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole likes the mirrors under the plates and the plain white draped tablecloths in the AI-generated wedding table designs.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I like the mirrors under the plates... I like the draping of the tablecloths. And I think they're plain white, which I like.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole believes the heavily candle-crowded wedding table design looks as though the couple was trying to save money by skipping flowers and then overcompensated with candles — not dramatic, just excessive.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I don't think it looks dramatic, it looks like a table crammed full of candles and I think to myself, why all the candles? This is very unusual. I don

claims.text: Catherine Cole disagrees with Mary Jo Cole's critique of the candle-heavy wedding table design and favors a 'more-is-more candles' maximalist aesthetic for impact.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I kinda disagree but I may be outnumbered here... The vision is more-is-more candles

claims.text: Catherine Cole found the original airier wedding table design with candles at different heights too generic and less impactful.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: It felt generic... Less impactful

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole prefers adding more flowers to the wedding table design to balance the candles, and likes the version with more calla lilies better.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I do like that better, with more flowers.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole believes good design benefits from white space and breathing room — analogous to silence in music — and applies this principle to the wedding table candle arrangement.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I think it's like with music and design generally - it helps to have white space (or silence) to allow it to breathe

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole suggests exploring a wedding table design with even fewer candles than the current options — something between the second picture and the last one — to see if it achieves better visual balance.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I think we should play with even fewer just to see what that might look like. So something between the second picture and the last one.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole strongly dislikes the 'bread face' wedding aesthetic and considers it ghoulish, awful, and completely inappropriate for a wedding.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: That is ghoulish and awful... I loathe bread faces for a wedding. I feel quite strongly about it

claims.text: Catherine Cole and Mary Jo Cole have an established pattern of disagreeing on aesthetic wedding choices — Catherine explicitly references the bread-face wedding as a prior example of divergent taste.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I fear this is a bread-face situation. I loved the bread-face wedding; you did not.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole acknowledges that a candle-heavy wedding table design does need a large quantity of candles to achieve impact, conceding that the underlying concept has merit even if current execution feels crowded.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I think for the multi-candle idea to have impact, you actually do have to have a lot of them.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole also noted that the original airier candle design may have felt less summery and more fall-like, which contributed to her dislike of it — not just the candle quantity.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: it may be because it looks way more fall-like than summery.

claims.text: Catherine Cole committed to sleeping on the wedding table candle design decision before making a final call.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: Okay I will sleep on this
claims.date_note: Implied overnight; likely resolved by morning of 2026-03-13

claims.text: Catherine Cole is using AI-generated image tools to iterate on wedding table designs, producing multiple variations exploring different candle quantities, flower arrangements, and chair styles.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I wrangled AI to produce something simpler with more candles, all at the same level, and wood chairs.

claims.text: Catherine Cole's wedding table design must account for drip-wax safety — Mary Jo Cole flagged the need to verify candles do not drip hot wax on guests.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: At some point, you'll need to get the candles and make sure they don't drip hot wax all over the place.

memory_writes.value: Favors a maximalist 'more-is-more candles' approach for dramatic impact in wedding table design; uses AI image generation to iterate on options.
Prefers bold, high-impact, maximalist design aesthetics; found the bread-face wedding appealing; found the airier candle design too generic.
Actively engaged in design decisions, using AI tools to generate mockups and iterating based on feedback; seeks input but holds strong personal aesthetic opinions.
Prefers fewer candles with more flowers and white space in wedding table design; values visual breathing room; strongly dislikes the bread-face wedding aesthetic.
Believes good design, like music, benefits from white space and silence to allow breathing room.
Believes most people hire wedding planners because they don't want to plan; sees Catherine's approach as using a planner as a supplement to her own planning.

Candle quantity and flower balance in table centerpiece design is unresolved as of 2026-03-12. Catherine sleeping on it. Mary Jo prefers fewer candles plus more flowers. Catherine prefers maximalist candles. Need to also verify candles won't drip hot wax.

claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: Thank you so much for the birthday gift! I loved the card so much—it's still sitting on my desk. And thank you for the money!

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole's birthday card to Stephen Andrews made a strong impression — he noted it is still sitting on his desk.
claims.subject: Stephen Andrews
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: I loved the card so much—it's still sitting on my desk.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole committed to Venmo-ing Stephen Andrews $175 for insurance, pending receipt of his Venmo name.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I can Venmo you $175 for the insurance. Just tell me what your name is on Venmo so it goes to the right person.
claims.date_note: Payment pending Stephen's reply with Venmo name
claims.condition_trigger: Stephen Andrews provides his Venmo username to Mary Jo Cole

claims.text: Stephen Andrews needs to provide Mary Jo Cole with his Venmo name so she can send him $175 for insurance reimbursement.
claims.subject: Stephen Andrews
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: Just tell me what your name is on Venmo so it goes to the right person.
claims.date_note: No deadline stated; action is a prerequisite for Mary Jo's payment

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole has a warm, generous relationship with Stephen Andrews, having given him a birthday gift and offering an insurance reimbursement, and dismissing his apology for delayed thanks.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: Well, you are welcome and of course no apology is needed!

memory_writes.value: Gave Stephen Andrews a birthday card and money as a gift; card made enough of an impression that he kept it on his desk.
Offered to Venmo Stephen Andrews $175 for insurance reimbursement, pending receipt of his Venmo username.
Needs to reply to Mary Jo Cole with his Venmo name so she can send $175 insurance reimbursement.

claims.text: The Senate Judiciary Committee has an Executive Business Meeting scheduled for March 19, 2026.
claims.subject: Senate Judiciary Committee
claims.speaker: Vijay Menon
claims.evidence: https://www.judiciary.senate.gov/committee-activity/hearings/executive-business-meeting-03-19-2026

claims.text: The GUARD bill will not be added to the Senate Judiciary Committee's Executive Business Meeting scheduled for March 19, 2026.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: No, it won't be added to next week's.

claims.text: There is an outside chance the GUARD bill could be listed on Monday March 16 to be held over, but the more likely scenario is markup the following week, which would push it to April.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: There's an outside chance it could get listed on Monday to be held over, but would probably be the following week
claims.date_note: Markup pushed to April if not listed week of March 16

claims.text: Will Simpson committed to going back to Senator Grassley with a whip count update and attempting to persuade him to list the GUARD bill for markup without waiting for White House OLA position feedback, once conversations with Senators Schmitt and Cornyn have occurred.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Once we talk to Schmitt and Cornyn I'll go back to Grassley with a whip count update and see if we can talk then into listing without WH feedback
claims.date_note: Depends on when Schmitt and Cornyn conversations happen
claims.condition_trigger: Completion of conversations with Senator Schmitt and Senator Cornyn about GUARD bill amendments

claims.text: The White House Office of Legislative Affairs (WH OLA) has been repeatedly telling Senator Grassley to wait one more week before announcing their position on the GUARD bill, and has been delaying for weeks.
claims.subject: White House Office of Legislative Affairs
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: WH OLA keeps telling Grassley to just wait one more week and they will let them know their position. Grassley's fear is WH opposition the day before a

claims.text: Senator Grassley fears that White House opposition announced the day before a GUARD bill markup would blow up the markup with drama.
claims.subject: Senator Grassley
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Grassley's fear is WH opposition the day before a markup that just blows it up with drama

claims.text: Vijay Menon believes the White House is weighing how aggressively to oppose the GUARD bill (AI/chatbot regulation).
claims.subject: Vijay Menon
claims.speaker: Vijay Menon
claims.evidence: I'm sure the WH is weighing how berserk they want to go here

claims.text: Vijay Menon flagged that the team will want Rule V to apply to the GUARD bill.
claims.subject: Vijay Menon
claims.speaker: Vijay Menon
claims.evidence: Smaller point, but we will want to have Rule V apply to our bill

claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Schmitt is still leaning no on the chatbot bill right now, very skeptical of AI regs in general

claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Ethan has a couple of ideas for amendments that he thinks might help. I think they are mostly doable, one is a harder ask. He is not going to draft th

claims.text: Will Simpson believes Ethan's proposed GUARD bill amendments are mostly doable, but one is a harder ask.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: I think they are mostly doable, one is a harder ask

claims.text: Will Simpson is uncertain how much to further edit the GUARD bill manager's amendment without a promise of Senator Schmitt's support, given that Schmitt is only providing conceptual amendment ideas and not drafting them.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: I'm not sure how much we want to further edit our manager's amendment without a promise of his support

claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: We can discuss more when you're back on monday.
